name: daily-news-report
description: Scrapes content based on a preset URL list, filters high-quality technical information, and generates daily Markdown reports.
capabilities: Scrapes content from 10+ curated tech news sources (Hacker News, HuggingFace Papers, Paul Graham Essays, etc.)
Filters and prioritizes high-quality technical content using quality scoring
Automatically deduplicates content using URL and title similarity checks
Generates well-formatted Markdown reports with summaries and key points
Supports parallel execution with sub-agents and graceful fallback to serial mode
Maintains cache to avoid re-processing previously covered articles
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate news aggregation tool that scrapes public websites and generates markdown reports. Content hashing uses MD5 for deduplication (not security), backticks are markdown code fences (not shell execution), hardcoded URLs are the core functionality, and environment checks are fallback mechanisms.
valueStatement: This skill automates gathering and curating daily tech news from multiple sources into a single organized Markdown report, saving hours of manual research time.
limitations: Depends on external news sources being accessible and not rate-limited
Browser rendering required for JavaScript-heavy sites (Product Hunt, Latent Space)
Quality of output depends on source availability and content freshness
No built-in support for custom source configuration through user prompts

useCases.title: Daily Research Brief for Developers
useCases.description: Software developers use this skill each morning to get a curated digest of the top tech news and articles without manually visiting multiple sites.

useCases.title: Team Knowledge Sharing
useCases.description: Team leads generate daily reports to share with their team, keeping everyone updated on the latest tech trends and tools.

useCases.targetUser: Tech content creators and newsletter authors
useCases.title: Content Curation for Newsletters
useCases.description: Content creators use the generated reports as source material for weekly tech newsletters or blog posts.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Daily Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the daily-news-report skill to generate today's tech news report.
promptTemplates.scenario: Get a quick daily digest of tech news

promptTemplates.title: Specific Date Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the daily-news-report skill with the argument [optional: 2026-02-20] to generate a report for a specific date.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate a report for a past date

promptTemplates.title: Filtered by Topic
promptTemplates.prompt: Run daily-news-report skill and prioritize content related to AI and machine learning in the final report.
promptTemplates.scenario: Get news focused on a specific topic

promptTemplates.title: Full Multi-Source Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute daily-news-report with all available sources including browser-based sources to generate a comprehensive report.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate the most comprehensive report possible

bestPractices: Run the skill early in the day when source websites have fresh content
Review the quality scores to understand how content was filtered
Check the cache to avoid duplicating content from previous reports
antiPatterns: Do not run the skill multiple times in one day - it will only produce duplicate content
Do not expect real-time news - there is a delay from publication to scraping
Do not rely on this for breaking news - it is designed for curated daily digests

faq.question: What sources does this skill scrape?
faq.answer: The skill scrapes from Hacker News, HuggingFace Papers, Paul Graham Essays, One Useful Thing, James Clear, Farnam Street, HackerNoon, Scott Young, Product Hunt, and Latent Space.

faq.question: How long does it take to generate a report?
faq.answer: Depending on source availability, it takes approximately 2-6 minutes. Tier 1 sources alone take about 2 minutes.

faq.question: Can I add my own news sources?
faq.answer: Currently, source configuration requires editing the sources.json file directly. Custom source addition is not supported through prompts.

faq.question: What happens if a source is unavailable?
faq.answer: The skill has fault tolerance - if one source fails, it continues with remaining sources and logs the error. Failed sources are marked in the configuration.

faq.question: Does the skill work without internet?
faq.answer: No, the skill requires internet access to scrape news sources. It will fail if external websites are unreachable.

faq.question: How does the quality scoring work?
faq.answer: Each source has an average quality score (3.8-4.8). Articles are scored based on source credibility, engagement metrics, and content relevance to tech topics.
